page: Microsoft Azure D8d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv4 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. Additionally, the server includes 307 GB of local storage, while offering no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the D8d_v4 consistently in the average tier for most workloads. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yield scores of 1421.78 and 7443.02 respectively, while the Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores reach 1237.0 and 4751.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 414.44 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 46.63 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression show weaker performance, scoring in the bottom 25% of tested systems.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and integrated 307 GB local storage, the D8d_v4 is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, and static web serving. It achieves 7.06 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 6.77 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. The inclusion of local storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for applications requiring fast temporary disk space without the overhead of external block storage, though the lack of GPU acceleration and weaker compression performance represent notable tradeoffs for heavy data processing or machine learning tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D8d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.6 GHz, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores with hyper-threading), 32.0 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage. Benchmark results place this server in the average tier for most operations, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 4751.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.06 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in compression tasks, such as single-threaded gzip compression. With its balanced resource allocation and built-in local storage, the D8d_v4 is designed for general-purpose applications, database operations, and static web serving, offering a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring local disk space without demanding high-end GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage, delivering balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8d_v4 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated local storage for standard enterprise workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
307 GB of integrated local storage for temporary data
Average performance in database operations and static web serving
Suitable for general-purpose applications, databases, and web servers
